customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, genuinely motivated customer. Prior solar evaluation with Ionic that 'wasn't the right fit' — he's comparison-shopping, not skeptical of solar itself. Comfortable with phone appointments. No scam anxiety surfaced.
unresolved_concerns: Wife's title status is unconfirmed — Jim said 'maybe my wife' but the appointment was booked without her. If she is on the title, the closer cannot close without her present.
Jim expressed interest in PPA/lease options specifically — Holland did not address this. Jim may be expecting a lease/PPA discussion, not a purchase proposal.
Prior Ionic experience 'wasn't the right fit' — closer should probe what specifically didn't work to avoid repeating the same friction.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior solar evaluation with Ionic (purchase option, didn't fit) — Jim is not a first-time prospect
Wife's involvement in the decision — Jim mentioned her twice but she is not on the appointment
exact_promises_rep_made: Guarantees savings within the first year of having solar
Jim will qualify for any state incentives if credit is above 670 — 'I'll make sure you get those'
Appointment is one hour blocked, typically 30-40 minutes
Text with appointment details will be sent after the call
closer_should_know: Jim's wife was not included in the appointment despite uncertain title status — confirm whether she is on the title before the call and, if so, reschedule to include her. Additionally, Holland made two ungrounded financial promises (savings guarantee, state incentives guarantee) that Jim will likely reference; the closer should address these early rather than let them sit as unmet expectations.
